overview:
The AZ-204 exam validates the skills required to design, build, test, and maintain cloud solutions on Microsoft Azure. It focuses on the development of compute solutions, storage implementation, security integration, and the consumption of third-party services. Candidates must demonstrate proficiency in Azure SDKs, REST APIs, and the Azure CLI.

This certification is intended for developers who are responsible for creating Azure applications. Prerequisites include a strong foundation in programming (C# or Python) and a basic understanding of cloud concepts, typically evidenced by the AZ-900 certification or equivalent professional experience.
recertification_info: Recertification is available for free via a shorter online renewal assessment on the Microsoft Learn platform, which must be completed annually after the first year.

official_resources.title: Microsoft Learn Path: Azure Developer Associate

preparation_tips: Focus heavily on Azure Functions, specifically the different triggers and bindings available.
Master the differences between App Service plans and the various scaling options (Scale-up vs Scale-out).
Practice implementing Azure Cosmos DB, focusing on consistency levels and partitioning strategies.
Deep dive into Azure Key Vault for managing secrets, keys, and certificates.
Understand the implementation of Managed Identities to avoid hardcoding credentials in code.
Learn the nuances of Azure Service Bus, Event Grid, and Event Hubs to know when to use each for messaging.
Study the Azure Cache for Redis implementation and common caching patterns.
Practice using the Azure CLI and PowerShell to automate resource deployment.
Review the implementation of Azure API Management (APIM) for securing and throttling APIs.
Build a small project that integrates multiple services to understand the end-to-end data flow.
exam_day_tips: Read the scenario-based questions carefully; the specific requirement (e.g., 'lowest cost' or 'minimum effort') often dictates the correct answer.
Manage your time strictly; if a coding snippet question is taking too long, mark it for review and move on.
Be prepared for case studies which provide a large amount of business and technical requirements before the questions.
Utilize the process of elimination for multiple-choice questions to narrow down the correct Azure service.
Ensure your system requirements are met if taking the exam remotely to avoid technical interruptions.
Pay attention to the specific SDK methods mentioned in the code-completion questions.
recommended_experience: At least 6 months of professional experience developing applications on Azure and proficiency in at least one modern programming language.
